signal_title: Harmonisation of Transparency Reporting Rules under the DSA
risk_rationale: The new EU regulation requires standardized transparency reporting under the DSA, which may necessitate adjustments to existing reporting practices but does not pose an existential threat.

compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Uniform reporting templates and periods as per the new EU Implementing Regulation.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current biannual reporting may need to be adjusted to align with the new standardized templates and reporting periods mandated by the EU.

remediations.title: Align EU Transparency Reporting with DSA Requirements
remediations.description: Update the transparency reporting process to comply with the new standardized templates and reporting periods as mandated by the EU Implementing Regulation.
acceptance_criteria: Transparency reports use the new standardized templates.
Reports are submitted according to the new standardized periods.

business_impact: Moderate impact due to the need for process adjustments in transparency reporting, which may require resource allocation and potential temporary disruption in reporting workflows.
recommended_actions: Review the new EU Implementing Regulation in detail to understand specific template and period requirements.
Coordinate with the EU Compliance team to implement necessary changes in the reporting process.
Communicate changes to relevant stakeholders to ensure awareness and compliance with the new requirements.
